perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Section 1.1(b), (i) the "Market Price" of any Products & Services shall mean the price for such Products & Services established by Constellation as the competitive market price at which such Products & Services are to be made available to Constellation's customers with a similar anticipated load profile within a specified utility service territory.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Section 1.1(b), (i) the "Market Price" of any Products & Services shall mean the price for such Products & Services as unilaterally determined by Constellation, without regard to competitive market pricing or the load profile of other customers.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Market Price.' The original definition specifies that Market Price is a 'competitive market price,' while the modified version allows Constellation to unilaterally determine the price without considering competitive market factors. This introduces an In-Text Contradiction with section 1.1 (a) which says '(a) The HOF Entities shall consult with Constellation to develop a comprehensive energy strategy with respect to the Products & Services listing in Exhibit A, which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The HOF Entities shall consult with Constellation to develop a comprehensive energy strategy with respect to the Products & Services listing in Exhibit A, which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing (incorporating completion of the Supplier Component Questionnaire in the form of Exhibit B hereof for the applicable HOF Entity or affiliate), periodic reporting and analytics, regulatory/market intelligence and infrastructure services and equipment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Market Price' from a competitive price to a price unilaterally determined by Constellation, disregarding competitive market factors. This directly contradicts section (a), which mandates a comprehensive energy strategy reflecting 'competitive market pricing.' The altered definition makes the original requirement in section (a) impossible to fulfill as written, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Constellation shall invoice the applicable HOF Entity or its appropriate affiliate for all Products & Services purchased by such HOF Entity or its appropriate affiliate pursuant to the applicable Second Level Agreement (as defined below).
perturbation.changed_text: Constellation shall invoice the HOF Entities only for Products & Services if those HOF entities have executed a Second Level Agreement; Products & Services supplied to an affiliate of an HOF Entity can be invoiced directly to the affiliate, irrespective of a Second Level Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the invoicing process. While the original text mandates that all invoices must be pursuant to a Second Level Agreement, the revised version allows for invoices directly to affiliates without such an agreement. This contradicts with the first sentense in section 1.1(d) 'All purchases of Products & Services from Constellation by the HOF Entities or their respective affiliates pursuant to this Agreement shall be made pursuant to a separate agreement executed between an HOF Entity or affiliate and Constellation which may include a master retail electricity supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-1, master retail natural gas supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-2, transaction confirmation in substantially the form of Exhibit C-3 or similar document or agreement (each, a "Second Level Agreement").'
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) All purchases of Products & Services from Constellation by the HOF Entities or their respective affiliates pursuant to this Agreement shall be made pursuant to a separate agreement executed between an HOF Entity or affiliate and Constellation which may include a master retail electricity supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-1, master retail natural gas supply agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit C-2, transaction confirmation in substantially the form of Exhibit C-3 or similar document or agreement (each, a "Second Level Agreement"). In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between this Agreement and any Second Level Agreement negotiated after execution of this Agreement and not substantially in the form attached hereto, this Agreement shall control except to the extent that the Second Level Agreement expressly refers to this Agreement and states that the specific provision thereof shall be deemed to amend and supersede a specifically identified provision hereof (in which case such specific provision of the Second Level Agreement shall control over such specifically identified provision hereof).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception regarding invoicing affiliates, allowing direct invoicing without a Second Level Agreement, while the contradicted text explicitly states that all purchases by HOF Entities or their affiliates must be made pursuant to a Second Level Agreement. This creates a direct conflict regarding the requirement of a Second Level Agreement for affiliate purchases, as outlined in section (d), thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Section 2.2 shall not be deemed to apply to agreements executed prior to the date of this Agreement between the HOF Entities and Johnson Controls, Inc. or any of its affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Section 2.2 shall also not be deemed to apply to agreements executed between the HOF Entities and any vendor providing renewable energy solutions, regardless of when those agreements were executed.
perturbation.explanation: This modification broadens the exception to the exclusivity clause. The original clause only exempted agreements with Johnson Controls executed before the agreement date. The new language extends the exception to agreements with ANY vendor providing renewable energy solutions, irrespective of the agreement's date. This contradict with section 2.2 clause (a) 'neither of the HOF Entities shall purchase, at any time during the Term, any commodity electricity or gas from any person or entity other than Constellation and its affiliates'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Provided that Constellation is not then in breach of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement, including without limitation Section 2.1 hereof, and provided that Constellation has available for purchase a product or service which meets the needs of the HOF Entities at competitive market pricing, (a) neither of the HOF Entities shall purchase, at any time during the Term, any commodity electricity or gas from any person or entity other than Constellation and its affiliates and (b) in the event and to the extent mutually agreed by the Parties, neither of the HOF Entities shall grant or award to any company designated by Constellation (and mutually agreeable to the HOF Entities) any project which the Parties mutually agree shall not be granted or awarded to such company. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Section 2.2 shall also not be deemed to apply to agreements executed between the HOF Entities and any vendor providing renewable energy solutions, regardless of when those agreements were executed.
perturbation.justification: The original text created an exception to the exclusivity clause only for agreements with Johnson Controls executed before the agreement date. The changed text expands this exception to include agreements with ANY vendor providing renewable energy solutions, regardless of when the agreement was executed. This contradicts the original intent of Section 2.2(a), which states that the HOF entities shall not purchase commodity electricity or gas from any entity other than Constellation and its affiliates, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.
